title: Dataset Agreement Rate
concept: Human labelers often disagree on quality; monitoring inter-annotator agreement ensures the reward model is trained on consistent preference signals.

exercises.prompt: When multiple humans rate the quality of AI responses, what usually happens?

options.text: They always agree 100% of the time.
They often disagree on which response is better.
They never look at the same responses.

exercises.explain: Human labelers frequently disagree on what makes a response 'good' because of personal preferences, cultural differences, or unclear guidelines.

cards.title: Do Humans Agree?
cards.body: When we train AI models, we ask humans to choose which AI answers are better. But humans do not always agree! If our human helpers are constantly arguing about the answers, our AI will get confused. Let's learn how we measure and handle this human disagreement.

cards.title: What is Dataset Agreement Rate?
cards.body: Dataset Agreement Rate measures how often different human annotators choose the same preferred AI response. If labelers constantly disagree, the training data becomes noisy, making it difficult for the reward model to learn what a good response actually looks like.

cards.title: The Food Critic Panel
cards.body: Imagine asking three food critics to rate a new burger. If all three say it is delicious, you have high agreement and can trust the rating. If one says it is amazing, one says it is terrible, and the third says it is just okay, their agreement is low. You cannot easily use their feedback to improve the recipe because their opinions are all over the place!

cards.title: Measuring Agreement
cards.body: A team of labelers reviews 100 pairs of AI responses. For 85 of those pairs, all labelers agree on which answer is better. For the other 15, they disagree. This dataset has an 85% agreement rate, which is generally considered strong and reliable for training a reward model.

cards.title: Myth: Human Ratings are Perfect
cards.body: It is tempting to think that human feedback is always correct. In reality, human preferences are highly subjective and noisy. Without measuring agreement rates, we might train our reward model on completely contradictory instructions!

cards.title: Clear Signals vs. Chaos
cards.body: Compare how the reward model reacts to consistent agreement versus chaotic disagreement.

right.text: Labelers contradict each other. The AI gets confused by mixed signals.

left.text: Labelers agree on what is good. The AI gets clear signals and learns quickly.

exercises.prompt: Why is a high dataset agreement rate important for training a reward model?

options.text: It ensures the model learns from consistent, reliable preference signals.
It makes the AI model train ten times faster.
It automatically deletes all bad responses from the internet.
It allows the AI to run without using any computer memory.

exercises.explain: If the agreement rate is high, it means the labelers share a unified understanding of what makes a response good, giving the reward model clear, consistent examples to learn from.

exercises.prompt: True or False: A high-quality human preference dataset must always have a perfect 100% agreement rate.
exercises.explain: Even with clear guidelines, human preferences are subjective, and perfect 100% agreement is extremely rare in real-world datasets.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the missing word to complete the concept.
exercises.sentence: If human labelers disagree often, the dataset will have a low _______ rate.
exercises.explain: A low agreement rate shows that the labelers are confused or have different opinions, resulting in noisy data.

exercises.prompt: Which of these approaches is the best way to improve a low dataset agreement rate?

options.text: Ignore the disagreement and hope the AI figures it out on its own.
Provide labelers with clear guidelines and examples of good responses.
Delete all data points where any disagreement occurred.

exercises.explain: Writing clear, detailed instructions helps human labelers understand exactly what to look for, which naturally increases their agreement rate.

exercises.prompt: Your labelers only agree on 45% of the comparisons because the instructions are too vague. How should you fix this to build a reliable reward model?

options.text: Keep training the reward model with the messy data anyway
Clarify the labeling guidelines to help the labelers align
Fire all the labelers and write the ratings yourself
Make the AI guess what the labelers meant to say

exercises.explain: A low agreement rate means labelers are arguing and cannot agree on which response is better. Training on this confusing data will make your AI model confused too. You must fix the instructions first to get clean, aligned feedback.
